body: Good catch on the 0.1. I worked my last three rounds by hand and they all land high by the same amount, so it's systematic. I'll trace the rounding step this weekend.
I can reproduce this with one of Owen's annotated games. Looks like the move parser doesn't expect a brace right after the SAN. Will see if I can skip the comment token for now.
Coordinates look right to me. One nit: White Sands switched from a monument to a park, so double-check the designation field before this merges.
Confirmed the column swap on the newer export. Mapping by header name instead of position fixes it. Thanks for attaching the sample row, that made it quick.
It's the conversion constant rounded to four places. Bumping it to the full factor lines the mph output up with the tables. Small fix, will push shortly.
A density-altitude input is a nice idea. For Scottsdale I'd default it to field elevation but let the user override. Daniel, does that fit how the calc is structured?
Same Arizona timezone gremlin as the logbook tool. Once I pin the offset to MST with no DST the moonrise lines up. Tested against the Tucson trip dates.
Verified the off-by-one on the Petrified Forest entry. Storing the local park date instead of UTC midnight cleared it up.
Agreed the gust factor is the useful bit for a tee time. I'll print 'G##KT' when the group is present and just the steady wind otherwise.
Sorting on the surname after stripping the prefix did the trick. 'von Richthofen' files under R now. Reading list is finally in order.
Found it — the script resolves the asset folder relative to the working directory. Anchoring it to the script path so I can run the tour preview from anywhere.
A --stations comma list would save me real time on cross-country planning. Happy to test it against my usual KSDL, KPHX, KDVT run once there's a branch.
Walked the gallery again with my notes and the Wright section really does come before the jet age wall. Reordering to match. Will push the corrected placard list.
Confirmed the return leg gets added twice on out-and-back routes. The 4Runner mileage reads right after the de-dup. Thanks Daniel for spotting where the loop double-adds.
Adding a shell guard so the zsh-only aliases don't error out under bash. Now it sources the right block for whichever shell I'm in.
Leaving myself a note: ask Daniel why strict-null flags this one. I think it wants optional chaining but I want to understand it before I just add a '?'.
Reproduced it on my end. The root cause is the timezone handling — we assume DST and Phoenix doesn't observe it. I'll have a fix up shortly.
Closing this — verified the fix in the latest build and the visibility group parses cleanly even when it's omitted. Thanks for the report.
Agreed, let's keep the scope tight. I'd rather ship a simple cache expiry than a full registry sync that I won't be able to maintain.
Tested against a few of my saved rounds and the blank-hole case no longer crashes. Marking this ready for review.
